skill.name: Azure Monitor Query SDK for Java
skill.description: Azure Monitor Query SDK for Java. Execute Kusto queries against Log Analytics workspaces and query metrics from Azure resources.
skill.summary: Query Azure Monitor logs and metrics using Java SDK with Kusto Query Language support.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 suspicious patterns detected. Manual review confirms this is a documentation-only skill providing usage guidance for the official Azure Monitor Query Java SDK. All code examples demonstrate legitimate SDK usage with proper credential handling via DefaultAzureCredentialBuilder. No executable code, network calls, or security concerns identified.

content.user_title: Query Azure Monitor logs and metrics with Java
content.value_statement: Developers need to analyze Azure resource telemetry and troubleshoot issues efficiently. This skill provides Kusto query patterns and metrics retrieval using the official Azure Monitor Query SDK for Java.
actual_capabilities: Execute Kusto queries against Log Analytics workspaces
Query Azure resource metrics with time-series aggregation
Support for synchronous and asynchronous client patterns
Batch multiple queries in a single request
Map query results to custom Java model classes
Configure sovereign cloud endpoints for Azure China
limitations: Package is deprecated; migration to azure-monitor-query-logs and azure-monitor-query-metrics recommended
Requires valid Azure credentials and workspace or resource permissions
Query complexity and result size subject to Azure Monitor service limits
Does not support writing or modifying log data, read-only queries only

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Troubleshooting Production Issues
use_cases.description: Query Log Analytics to investigate application errors, trace request failures, and identify performance bottlenecks across Azure resources.

use_cases.title: Platform Team Building Monitoring Dashboards
use_cases.description: Extract metrics time-series data for custom dashboards, alerts, and capacity planning reports using programmatic SDK access.

use_cases.title: Security Analyst Investigating Incidents
use_cases.description: Query AzureActivity logs and security-related tables to detect anomalous behavior and audit access patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Log Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Kusto query to find all errors in the last 24 hours from my Log Analytics workspace using the Azure Monitor Query Java SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Metrics Aggregation
prompt_templates.prompt: Query CPU percentage and memory metrics for my VM scale set with hourly aggregation over the past week using MetricsQueryClient.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Query Multiple Workspaces
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Java example that runs the same Kusto query across three different Log Analytics workspaces and combines the results.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Model Mapping with Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to map LogsQueryResult to a custom Java class with proper handling for PARTIAL_FAILURE and HttpResponseException.

output_examples.input: Query workspace for top 10 resource groups by activity count
output_examples.output: Console output showing ResourceGroup names with corresponding activity counts, sorted descending

output_examples.input: Retrieve SuccessfulCalls and TotalCalls metrics for API Management resource
output_examples.output: Time-series data printed with timestamps and metric values for each time interval

output_examples.input: Batch query with one failing query in the set
output_examples.output: Results for successful queries displayed; failure message and error details for the failed query

best_practices: Use batch queries to combine multiple queries into a single request and reduce latency
Set appropriate server timeouts for long-running queries and always check query result status for partial failures
Limit result size using Kusto operators like top or take, and project only needed columns
anti_patterns: Running unbounded queries without time restrictions that may exceed service limits or timeout
Ignoring PARTIAL_FAILURE status and assuming all query results are complete
Hardcoding workspace IDs or resource IDs instead of using environment variables or configuration

faq.question: What authentication method does this SDK use?
faq.answer: The SDK uses TokenCredential through DefaultAzureCredentialBuilder, supporting managed identity, environment credentials, CLI credentials, and other Azure authentication methods.

faq.question: Why is this package deprecated?
faq.answer: Microsoft split the functionality into two focused packages: azure-monitor-query-logs for Log Analytics queries and azure-monitor-query-metrics for metrics queries. Migration guides are available.

faq.question: Can I query multiple workspaces in a single request?
faq.answer: Yes, use LogsQueryOptions with setAdditionalWorkspaces to include up to 10 workspaces in a cross-workspace query.

faq.question: How do I handle large result sets?
faq.answer: Use Kusto query operators like take, top, or limit to restrict rows. Consider pagination patterns for very large datasets and cache results when appropriate.

faq.question: Does this support Azure sovereign clouds?
faq.answer: Yes, configure custom endpoints using the builder endpoint method for Azure China Cloud or other sovereign cloud environments.

faq.question: What is the difference between LogsQueryClient and LogsQueryAsyncClient?
faq.answer: LogsQueryClient provides blocking synchronous calls suitable for simple scripts. LogsQueryAsyncClient returns reactive types for non-blocking operations in high-throughput applications.
